title: Severance (TV series)
post_march_2025_fact: Season 2 concluded on March 20, 2025 on Apple TV+ and was renewed for a third season; the show became Apple TV+'s most-watched series.

title: Squid Game season 3
post_march_2025_fact: The third and final season of Squid Game premiered on Netflix on June 27, 2025 with all six episodes; Cate Blanchett appears in the finale as an American recruiter.

title: The Last of Us (TV series)
post_march_2025_fact: Season 2 premiered on April 13, 2025 and concluded on May 25, 2025; HBO renewed the series for a third season on April 9, 2025.

title: Andor (TV series)
post_march_2025_fact: The second and final season premiered on April 22, 2025 with three episodes per week, concluding May 13, 2025.

title: Stranger Things
post_march_2025_fact: The fifth and final season was released by Netflix in three volumes in November and December 2025, with the finale on December 31, 2025.

title: The Bear (TV series)
post_march_2025_fact: Season 4 of The Bear was released on June 25, 2025 on FX on Hulu.

title: Wednesday (TV series)
post_march_2025_fact: Season 2 premiered on August 6, 2025 with the second half released September 3, 2025.

title: Squid Game: The Challenge
post_march_2025_fact: Season 2 was released on Netflix in three batches across November 2025; Player 072 (Perla Figuereo) won the season.

title: Solo Leveling (TV series)
post_march_2025_fact: The second season, titled Solo Leveling: Arise from the Shadow, aired from January 5 to March 30, 2025.

title: Demon Slayer: Kimetsu no Yaiba – The Movie: Infinity Castle
post_march_2025_fact: The film, the first of a trilogy adapting the Infinity Castle arc, was released in Japan on July 18, 2025 and grossed over $781 million worldwide.

title: Spy × Family (TV series)
post_march_2025_fact: Season 3 of the anime aired from October 4 to December 27, 2025.

title: The Apothecary Diaries
post_march_2025_fact: The 24-episode second anime season aired from January 10 to July 4, 2025.

title: One Piece (1999 TV series)
post_march_2025_fact: The anime moved to a Sunday night primetime slot starting April 6, 2025 and was announced to go on hiatus from January to March 2026 with a new two-cours-per-year structure.

title: Chainsaw Man
post_march_2025_fact: Chainsaw Man – The Movie: Reze Arc premiered in Japan on September 19, 2025 and an Assassins Arc anime was announced at Jump Festa '26.

title: My Hero Academia
post_march_2025_fact: The eighth and final season of the anime aired from October 4 to December 13, 2025.

title: Dandadan
post_march_2025_fact: The 12-episode second season of the anime aired from July 4 to September 19, 2025; a third season was announced for 2027.

title: Sakamoto Days
post_march_2025_fact: The anime's two cours aired in 2025 (January-March and July-September), and it became Netflix's most-watched anime in the first half of 2025; a second season was announced.

title: Bon Appétit, Your Majesty
post_march_2025_fact: The Korean fantasy-romance drama starring Lim Yoona aired on tvN from August 23 to September 28, 2025 and ranked first in 23 countries on Netflix.

title: Adolescence (TV series)
post_march_2025_fact: The four-episode Netflix limited series, each shot in a single take, premiered March 13, 2025 and won 9 awards including Outstanding Limited Series at the 77th Primetime Emmy Awards.

title: The Studio (TV series)
post_march_2025_fact: The Apple TV+ Hollywood satire premiered March 26, 2025, won Outstanding Comedy Series at the 77th Primetime Emmy Awards, and was renewed for a second season.

title: Daredevil: Born Again
post_march_2025_fact: The Disney+ series premiered March 4, 2025 with Charlie Cox returning as Matt Murdock; nine-episode first season concluded in April 2025.

title: Hacks (TV series)
post_march_2025_fact: Season 4 aired from April 10 to May 29, 2025 on HBO Max, with Jean Smart winning her fourth consecutive Emmy for Lead Actress in a Comedy.

title: Black Mirror
post_march_2025_fact: Season 7 of Black Mirror was released on Netflix on April 10, 2025 and included USS Callister: Into Infinity, the show's first direct sequel episode.

title: The Pitt (TV series)
post_march_2025_fact: Season 1 finale aired April 10, 2025 on Max; the medical drama was renewed for a second season and won Outstanding Drama Series at the 77th Primetime Emmys.

title: Foundation (TV series)
post_march_2025_fact: Season 3 premiered on Apple TV+ on July 11, 2025 and concluded September 12, 2025; the series was renewed for a fourth season.

title: Alien: Earth
post_march_2025_fact: The Noah Hawley-created Alien prequel series premiered on FX on August 12, 2025 and was renewed for a second season in November 2025.

title: Peacemaker (TV series)
post_march_2025_fact: Season 2 premiered on HBO Max on August 21, 2025 and concluded October 9, 2025; James Gunn confirmed there are no plans for a third season.

title: The Sandman (TV series)
post_march_2025_fact: Season 2, the show's final season, was released across two parts on July 3 and July 24, 2025 with a special concluding episode on July 31, 2025.

title: Murderbot (TV series)
post_march_2025_fact: The Apple TV+ adaptation of Martha Wells' Murderbot Diaries starring Alexander Skarsgård premiered May 16, 2025 and was renewed for a second season.

title: The Wheel of Time (TV series)
post_march_2025_fact: Amazon Prime Video cancelled the series after three seasons in May 2025 despite strong critical reception for season 3.

title: The Simpsons
post_march_2025_fact: On April 2, 2025 the show was renewed for four additional seasons on Fox, extending the run to 858 episodes.

title: South Park
post_march_2025_fact: Season 27 premiered July 23, 2025 after a delay; the show signed a new five-year exclusive streaming deal with Paramount+ in mid-2025.

title: Invincible (TV series)
post_march_2025_fact: Amazon renewed the animated series for a fifth season in July 2025; season 3 concluded March 13, 2025.

title: Ironheart (miniseries)
post_march_2025_fact: The Marvel Studios miniseries with Dominique Thorne premiered on Disney+ on June 24, 2025 with all six episodes released by July 1, 2025.

title: Doctor Who
post_march_2025_fact: Ncuti Gatwa departed at the end of his second season on May 31, 2025, with Billie Piper introduced as the next incarnation of the Doctor.

title: Ted Lasso
post_march_2025_fact: In March 2025 the show was officially greenlit for a fourth season, with Ted coaching a women's team.

title: The Boys (TV series)
post_march_2025_fact: Filming for the fifth and final season wrapped on June 21, 2025, with the season premiering in April 2026.

title: Only Murders in the Building
post_march_2025_fact: Season 5 premiered September 9, 2025 on Hulu with new cast Renée Zellweger and Christoph Waltz; renewed for a sixth season set in London.

title: Slow Horses
post_march_2025_fact: The fifth series, adapting Mick Herron's London Rules, premiered on Apple TV+ on September 24, 2025 and concluded October 29, 2025.

title: Outer Banks (TV series)
post_march_2025_fact: Filming on the fifth and final season began June 20, 2025 and wrapped December 19, 2025 ahead of a 2026 premiere.

title: Bridgerton
post_march_2025_fact: Netflix announced a dual renewal of seasons 5 and 6 in May 2025, with Francesca confirmed as the lead for Season 5.

title: Emily in Paris
post_march_2025_fact: Season 5 premiered December 18, 2025, relocating Emily to Rome; assistant director Diego Borella died during filming in Venice in August 2025.

title: RuPaul's Drag Race
post_march_2025_fact: Season 17 concluded April 19, 2025 with Onya Nurve crowned the winner of a $250,000 grand prize.

title: Survivor (American TV series)
post_march_2025_fact: Kyle Fraser won Survivor 48 on May 21, 2025, and Savannah Louie won Survivor 49 on December 17, 2025.

title: Top Chef
post_march_2025_fact: Top Chef: Destination Canada (Season 22) concluded June 12, 2025 with Tristen Epps-Long crowned the winner.

title: Love Island (American TV series)
post_march_2025_fact: Season 7 ran from June 3 to August 25, 2025; Amaya Espinal and Bryan Arenales were crowned the winning couple.

title: Big Brother (American TV series)
post_march_2025_fact: Season 27, the show's 25-year anniversary season, premiered July 10, 2025 and ran through September 28, 2025; Ashley Hollis won the $750,000 grand prize.

title: MasterChef (American TV series)
post_march_2025_fact: Season 15 (Dynamic Duos) introduced pair-based competition for the first time; aired May 21 to September 17, 2025 with Jessica Bosworth and Jesse Rosenwald winning.

title: Saturday Night Live
post_march_2025_fact: A three-hour primetime live broadcast celebrating SNL's 50th anniversary aired on February 16, 2025; the show continued into the post-March-2025 50th season.

title: Hazbin Hotel
post_march_2025_fact: Season 2 premiered October 29, 2025 on Amazon Prime Video and concluded November 19, 2025; the show is renewed through a planned fifth and final season.

title: Helluva Boss
post_march_2025_fact: In April 2025 the series struck a deal to make uncensored episodes of seasons 1 and 2 available on Amazon Prime Video, with new seasons premiering on Prime Video first.

title: Smiling Friends
post_march_2025_fact: Season 3 premiered October 5, 2025 on Adult Swim with episodes airing weekly through April 2026.

title: SpongeBob SquarePants
post_march_2025_fact: Season 16 of SpongeBob SquarePants premiered on June 27, 2025 on Nickelodeon during the show's 25th anniversary year.

title: American Horror Story
post_march_2025_fact: In October 2025 the cast for Season 13 was announced, including Sarah Paulson, Evan Peters, Jessica Lange, and Ariana Grande.

title: Pluribus (TV series)
post_march_2025_fact: Vince Gilligan's Apple TV post-apocalyptic drama starring Rhea Seehorn premiered November 7, 2025; Seehorn won Best Actress in Drama at the Golden Globes.

title: Dexter: Resurrection
post_march_2025_fact: The Dexter sequel series premiered July 11, 2025 on Paramount+ with Showtime, with Michael C. Hall returning; renewed for a second season in October 2025.

title: Task (TV series)
post_march_2025_fact: Brad Ingelsby's HBO crime drama starring Mark Ruffalo premiered September 7, 2025 with seven episodes; renewed for a second season with Mahershala Ali joining the cast.

title: Sirens (2025 TV series)
post_march_2025_fact: The Netflix dark-comedy limited series adapted from Molly Smith Metzler's play premiered on May 22, 2025 with five episodes.

title: Long Story Short (TV series)
post_march_2025_fact: Raphael Bob-Waksberg's animated comedy-drama premiered on Netflix on August 22, 2025 and was renewed for a second season before launch.

title: Beyond the Bar
post_march_2025_fact: The Korean legal drama starring Lee Jin-wook premiered on JTBC on August 2, 2025 and was renewed for a second season in April 2026.

title: Typhoon Family
post_march_2025_fact: The Korean period drama starring Lee Jun-ho about the 1997 financial crisis aired on tvN from October 11 to November 30, 2025.

title: Wind Breaker (manga)
post_march_2025_fact: Season 2 of the Wind Breaker anime aired from April 4 to June 20, 2025 with 13 episodes.

title: Fire Force
post_march_2025_fact: The first cours of the third and final anime season aired from April 5 to June 21, 2025 on the Animeism block.

title: Witch Watch
post_march_2025_fact: The 25-episode Witch Watch anime aired from April 6 to October 5, 2025; a second season was announced after the finale.

title: Dr. Stone
post_march_2025_fact: The fourth and final anime season, Dr. Stone: Science Future, aired its first cours January-March 2025 and second cours July-September 2025.

title: Kaiju No. 8
post_march_2025_fact: Season 2 of the anime aired from July 19 to September 27, 2025; the original manga concluded with chapter 129 on July 18, 2025.

title: When Life Gives You Tangerines
post_march_2025_fact: The Korean drama released its 16 episodes on Netflix in four weekly volumes through March 28, 2025.

title: Kpop Demon Hunters
post_march_2025_fact: The Sony Pictures Animation/Netflix musical fantasy debuted June 20, 2025 and became the most-watched original title in Netflix history with over 500 million views.

title: 2025 Formula One World Championship
post_march_2025_fact: Lando Norris won his first drivers' championship for McLaren, ending Max Verstappen's four-year title reign; McLaren clinched the constructors' championship at Singapore.

title: WrestleMania 41
post_march_2025_fact: WrestleMania 41 was held April 19-20, 2025 in Las Vegas; John Cena defeated Cody Rhodes for a record 17th world title and Seth Rollins won the Night 1 main event.

title: 2025 NBA Finals
post_march_2025_fact: The Oklahoma City Thunder defeated the Indiana Pacers 4-3 to win their first championship in OKC, with Shai Gilgeous-Alexander named Finals MVP after Tyrese Haliburton tore his Achilles in Game 7.

title: 2025 World Series
post_march_2025_fact: The Los Angeles Dodgers defeated the Toronto Blue Jays 4-3 to win their second consecutive World Series title; Yoshinobu Yamamoto was named MVP.

title: 2025 Stanley Cup Finals
post_march_2025_fact: The Florida Panthers defeated the Edmonton Oilers 4-2 to win their second consecutive Stanley Cup; Sam Bennett earned the Conn Smythe Trophy.

title: 2025 Wimbledon Championships
post_march_2025_fact: Jannik Sinner won his first Wimbledon men's singles title and Iga Świątek won the women's title 6-0, 6-0; the tournament debuted automated electronic line judges.

title: 2025 French Open
post_march_2025_fact: Carlos Alcaraz beat Jannik Sinner in the longest French Open final ever (5h29m); Coco Gauff defeated Aryna Sabalenka to become the first American woman to win since Serena Williams in 2015.

title: 2025 US Open (tennis)
post_march_2025_fact: Carlos Alcaraz defeated defending champion Jannik Sinner for the men's title and Aryna Sabalenka defended her women's title against Amanda Anisimova.

title: 2025 Masters Tournament
post_march_2025_fact: Rory McIlroy beat Justin Rose in a playoff to win the Masters and complete the career Grand Slam, becoming the sixth player and first European to do so.

title: 2025 PGA Championship
post_march_2025_fact: Scottie Scheffler won his first PGA Championship by five strokes at Quail Hollow, his third major title overall.

title: 2025 U.S. Open (golf)
post_march_2025_fact: J.J. Spaun won his first major championship at Oakmont, beating Robert MacIntyre by two strokes after a final-round comeback.

title: 2025 Open Championship
post_march_2025_fact: Scottie Scheffler won his first Open Championship at Royal Portrush by four strokes over Harris English, claiming his fourth major.

title: 2025 FIFA Club World Cup
post_march_2025_fact: Chelsea defeated Paris Saint-Germain 3-0 to win the inaugural expanded 32-team FIFA Club World Cup hosted in the United States.

title: 2024–25 UEFA Champions League
post_march_2025_fact: Paris Saint-Germain won their first Champions League title with a record 5-0 victory over Inter Milan in Munich on May 31, 2025.

title: 2025 Indianapolis 500
post_march_2025_fact: Álex Palou won his first Indianapolis 500 on May 25, 2025, the first race featuring hybrid powertrains at Indy and the first broadcast on Fox.

title: 2025 Kentucky Derby
post_march_2025_fact: Sovereignty, trained by Bill Mott and ridden by Junior Alvarado, won the 151st Kentucky Derby on May 3, 2025 in sloppy conditions.

title: 2025 Preakness Stakes
post_march_2025_fact: Journalism, ridden by Umberto Rispoli, won the 150th Preakness Stakes on May 17, 2025 at Pimlico.

title: 2025 Belmont Stakes
post_march_2025_fact: Sovereignty won the 157th Belmont Stakes at Saratoga on June 7, 2025, the first colt since Justify in 2018 to win two Triple Crown legs.

title: 2025 Tour de France
post_march_2025_fact: Tadej Pogačar won his fourth Tour de France title for UAE Team Emirates XRG, with Florian Lipowitz becoming the first German on the podium since 2006.

title: Money in the Bank (2025)
post_march_2025_fact: Held June 7, 2025 at Intuit Dome; Seth Rollins and Naomi won the men's and women's Money in the Bank ladder matches respectively.

title: Survivor Series WarGames (2025)
post_march_2025_fact: Held November 29, 2025 at Petco Park, the first outdoor Survivor Series; The Vision team and Team Rhea won the WarGames matches; John Cena's final PPV.

title: The International 2025
post_march_2025_fact: Team Falcons defeated Xtreme Gaming 3-2 to win The International 2025 in Hamburg, Germany, the first time Germany has hosted since 2011.

title: 2025 League of Legends World Championship
post_march_2025_fact: T1 defeated KT Rolster 3-2 to win their third consecutive Worlds title in China; the inaugural Fearless Draft format was used.

title: 2025 Mid-Season Invitational
post_march_2025_fact: Gen.G defeated T1 3-2 in Vancouver to win their second consecutive MSI title; Chovy was named tournament MVP.

title: 2025 Call of Duty League season
post_march_2025_fact: OpTic Texas defeated Vancouver Surge 5-3 in the Grand Finals on June 29, 2025 in Kitchener; Mason 'Mercules' Ramsey won Finals MVP.

title: 2025 NWSL season
post_march_2025_fact: Gotham FC defeated Washington Spirit 1-0 in the final on November 22, 2025 with a goal from Rose Lavelle; Temwa Chawinga won league MVP.

title: UEFA Women's Euro 2025
post_march_2025_fact: England defended their European title with a penalty shootout win over Spain in the final, the first time the title was decided by shootout since 1984.

title: 2024–25 Premier League
post_march_2025_fact: Liverpool clinched their second Premier League title with four games to spare, tying Manchester United's record of 20 English league titles.

title: 2025–26 Premier League
post_march_2025_fact: Arsenal won the 2025-26 Premier League title; Burnley and Wolves were relegated; Erling Haaland topped scoring with 25 goals.

title: 2025 World Athletics Championships
post_march_2025_fact: Held in Tokyo September 13-21, 2025; Armand Duplantis set a pole vault world record of 6.30m and 53 nations won medals, the most ever.

title: 2025 World Aquatics Championships
post_march_2025_fact: Held in Singapore from July 11 to August 3, 2025, the first World Aquatics Championships hosted in Southeast Asia.

title: 2025 UCI Road World Championships
post_march_2025_fact: Held in Kigali, Rwanda September 21-28, 2025, the first UCI Road World Championships ever hosted in Africa; Tadej Pogačar won the men's road race.

title: 2025 Women's Rugby World Cup
post_march_2025_fact: Held in England from August 22 to September 27, 2025, with the final at Twickenham.

title: UFC 316
post_march_2025_fact: Held June 7, 2025 in Newark; Merab Dvalishvili submitted Sean O'Malley to retain the bantamweight title and Kayla Harrison submitted Julianna Peña.

title: UFC 320
post_march_2025_fact: Held October 4, 2025 in Las Vegas; Alex Pereira knocked out Magomed Ankalaev in 1:20 of round 1 to reclaim the light heavyweight title.

title: 2025 Major League Baseball season
post_march_2025_fact: First MLB season since 2005 with no no-hitter; Jen Pawol became the first woman to umpire a regular-season MLB game on August 9, 2025.

title: 2025 Monaco Grand Prix
post_march_2025_fact: Lando Norris won his first Monaco Grand Prix on May 25, 2025, McLaren's first Monaco victory since 2008.

title: 2025 Italian Grand Prix
post_march_2025_fact: Max Verstappen won the September 7, 2025 Italian GP at Monza, which became the fastest race in Formula One history.

title: 2025 British Grand Prix
post_march_2025_fact: Lando Norris won his first British Grand Prix on July 6, 2025; Nico Hülkenberg got his maiden podium at his 239th start, a new record.

title: 2025 São Paulo Grand Prix
post_march_2025_fact: Lando Norris won the 2025 São Paulo Grand Prix on November 9 from pole, taking both sprint and main race; Verstappen finished third from a pit-lane start.

title: 2025 FIFA U-20 World Cup
post_march_2025_fact: Morocco won their first FIFA tournament title by beating Argentina in the final, hosted in Chile from September 27 to October 19, 2025.

title: 2024–25 NBA season
post_march_2025_fact: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander won regular-season MVP, scoring title (32.7 ppg) and Finals MVP as the Thunder won the championship.

title: 2025 NBA draft
post_march_2025_fact: Held June 25-26, 2025 at Barclays Center; Cooper Flagg was selected first overall by the Dallas Mavericks.

title: 2025 NHL Entry Draft
post_march_2025_fact: Held June 27-28, 2025 in Los Angeles; Matthew Schaefer was picked first by the New York Islanders.

title: 2025 NFL Draft
post_march_2025_fact: Held April 24-26, 2025 at Lambeau Field; Cam Ward was selected first overall by the Tennessee Titans.

title: 2025–26 NBA season
post_march_2025_fact: First season under new media deals with NBC, ESPN/ABC and Amazon, ending the long TNT partnership; Houston acquired Kevin Durant in the largest trade in NBA history.

title: 2025 NFL season
post_march_2025_fact: Defending champion Eagles opened the season beating the Cowboys on September 4, 2025; the Seattle Seahawks won Super Bowl LX over New England.

title: Super Bowl LX
post_march_2025_fact: Played February 8, 2026 at Levi's Stadium; the Seattle Seahawks defeated the New England Patriots 29-13.

title: SummerSlam (2025)
post_march_2025_fact: Held August 2-3, 2025 at MetLife Stadium, the first two-night SummerSlam; CM Punk won the World title before Seth Rollins cashed in MITB; Cody Rhodes beat John Cena.

title: Crown Jewel (2025)
post_march_2025_fact: Held October 11, 2025 in Perth, the first Crown Jewel held outside Saudi Arabia; Seth Rollins beat Cody Rhodes for the men's Crown Jewel Championship.

title: Backlash (2025)
post_march_2025_fact: Held May 10, 2025 in St. Louis; John Cena retained the Undisputed WWE Championship against Randy Orton.

title: Night of Champions (2025)
post_march_2025_fact: Held June 28, 2025 in Riyadh; John Cena beat CM Punk to retain the Undisputed WWE Championship; Cody Rhodes won the King of the Ring tournament.

title: UFC 315
post_march_2025_fact: Held May 10, 2025 in Montreal; Jack Della Maddalena beat Belal Muhammad to win the welterweight title and Valentina Shevchenko retained the women's flyweight title against Manon Fiorot.

title: UFC 317
post_march_2025_fact: Held June 28, 2025 at T-Mobile Arena; Ilia Topuria knocked out Charles Oliveira in round 1 to win the vacant lightweight title.

title: Forbidden Door (2025)
post_march_2025_fact: Held August 24, 2025 at The O2 Arena in London, the first Forbidden Door held outside North America; Hangman Page retained the AEW World Championship over MJF.

title: G1 Climax 35
post_march_2025_fact: NJPW's annual round-robin tournament ran July 19 to August 17, 2025; Konosuke Takeshita defeated Evil in the final to win.

title: StarLadder Budapest Major 2025
post_march_2025_fact: Team Vitality defeated FaZe Clan 3-1 in the first best-of-five Major final; ZywOo earned his third Major MVP, the most of any CS player.

title: 2025 AFL Grand Final
post_march_2025_fact: Brisbane Lions beat Geelong 18.14 (122) to 11.9 (75) on September 27, 2025 for back-to-back premierships; Will Ashcroft won his second straight Norm Smith Medal.

title: 2025 NRL Grand Final
post_march_2025_fact: Brisbane Broncos beat Melbourne Storm 26-22 on October 5, 2025 for their first premiership since 2006; Reece Walsh won the Clive Churchill Medal.

title: 2025 Indian Premier League
post_march_2025_fact: Royal Challengers Bengaluru won their maiden IPL title beating Punjab Kings by 6 runs in the final; the tournament was suspended in May during the India-Pakistan crisis.

title: 2025 World Snooker Championship
post_march_2025_fact: Zhao Xintong became the first World Snooker Champion from China and Asia, beating Mark Williams 18-12 at the Crucible.

title: 2024–25 NHL season
post_march_2025_fact: Alexander Ovechkin scored his 895th goal on April 6, 2025 to break Wayne Gretzky's all-time NHL goal record.

title: 2025 World Series of Darts
post_march_2025_fact: Michael van Gerwen beat Luke Littler 11-7 in the World Series Finals in Amsterdam; Stephen Bunting topped the Order of Merit.

title: 2025 World Figure Skating Championships
post_march_2025_fact: Held March 26-30, 2025 at TD Garden in Boston; Ilia Malinin won men's and Alysa Liu won women's, the first US woman to do so since 2006.

title: 2025 Boston Marathon
post_march_2025_fact: Run April 21, 2025; John Korir won the men's race and Sharon Lokedi set a new women's course record (2:17:22).

title: 2025 London Marathon
post_march_2025_fact: Run April 27, 2025; Sabastian Sawe won the men's, Tigst Assefa set a women-only marathon record, and 56,640 finishers set a Guinness World Record.

title: 2025 New York City Marathon
post_march_2025_fact: Run November 2, 2025; Benson Kipruto won the men's by 0.03 seconds, the closest finish in race history; Hellen Obiri broke the women's course record.

title: 2025 World Series of Poker
post_march_2025_fact: Michael Mizrachi won the $10,000 Main Event for $10 million on July 16, 2025, becoming the first player to win both the Main Event and Poker Players Championship in the same year.

title: 2025 FIBA AmeriCup
post_march_2025_fact: Held in Managua, Nicaragua from August 22-31, 2025; Brazil beat Argentina 55-47 to win their fifth title, first in 16 years.

title: 2025 FIBA Asia Cup
post_march_2025_fact: Held in Jeddah from August 5-17, 2025; Australia beat China 90-89 to win their third title; Jaylin Galloway named MVP.

title: EuroBasket 2025
post_march_2025_fact: Germany beat Turkey 88-83 in the final to win their second EuroBasket title, 32 years after their first; Dennis Schröder named MVP.

title: 2025 World Surf League
post_march_2025_fact: Yago Dora and Molly Picklum won their first world titles at the WSL Finals, held for the first time at Cloudbreak in Fiji.

title: 2025 BWF World Championships
post_march_2025_fact: Held in Paris August 25-31, 2025; Shi Yuqi won men's singles and Akane Yamaguchi won women's singles; Canada won its first World Championship medal.
